examples:
Command Example
#
!exabeam-get-sequence-eventtypes asset_sequence_id=asset_sequence_id search_str="search_str"
exabeam-list-incident
#
Returns incidents from Exabeam.
Base Command
#

!exabeam-get-sequence-eventtypes asset_sequence_id=asset_sequence_id search_str="search_str"
exabeam-list-incident
#
Returns incidents from Exabeam.
Base Command
#
exabeam-list-incident
Input

Command Example
#
!exabeam-list-incident priority=high
Troubleshooting
#
If you are experiencing error 400 with the message "Internal Error: attempt to access uninitialized field" when trying to fetch incidents or search for a specific incident, we recommend using the user email in the username configuration with the prefix [saml].
For example, for the email
user@example.com

!exabeam-list-incident priority=high
Troubleshooting
#
If you are experiencing error 400 with the message "Internal Error: attempt to access uninitialized field" when trying to fetch incidents or search for a specific incident, we recommend using the user email in the username configuration with the prefix [saml].
For example, for the email
user@example.com
, use [saml]
user@example.com

outputs.description: The ID of the incident. Exabeam.incidents.name The name of the incident.
outputs.name: Exabeam.incidents.incidentId

outputs.description: Date The starting date of the incident. Exabeam.incidents.fields.closedDate
outputs.name: Exabeam.incidents.fields.startedDate

outputs.description: The ending date of the incident. Exabeam.incidents.fields.createdAt Date
outputs.name: Date
The creation date of the incident.

outputs.description: The incident status. Exabeam.incidents.fields.incidentType The incident type.
outputs.name: String

outputs.description: The incident source. Exabeam.incidents.fields.priority The incident priority.
outputs.name: Exabeam.incidents.fields.source

outputs.description: The incident queue. Exabeam.incidents.fields.description The incident description.
outputs.name: Exabeam.incidents.fields.queue
